item.title: An open letter to the author.
item.content:encoded:
Dear Hover Cat
The castle bravo test was 15Mt and could be seen from 250 mi (450km) away with fallout spreading 100mi (150 km), that could start a nuclear war in a matter of minutes if it went off anywhere but the middle of the ocean but as it is, there’s no hiding it.
I would suggest lowering the yield to 250-1000 lbs (125-250 kgs) of tnt, which would be more than enough to bring down any building, without letting the whole world know.
The ending feels kind of… rushed and out of place. You can either add more build up to the current ending, or completely scrap it and do something with an interview log with a -B instance. I am more inclined towards the later, but you’re the author.
There ate numerous spelling mistakes as well, but getting a friend to proofread this will easily fix that.
I’m hoping to see this in the recent changes section soon, but as the article is, it very well maybe put up for deletion soon.
Hoping that isn’t the case,
Agent Lackman

item.title: A small problem
item.content:encoded: There are a number of scp-# instances throughout the file.

item.title: Re: What a day!
item.content:encoded: Thanks, man! And I had a feeling it had something to do with phones or TVs. But I was listening to a podcast through shortwave radio and I thought, it could be cool if it was a radio. And the containment procedure had a lot of meat for me to work with, which helped me a lot.

item.title: Re: What a day!
item.content:encoded: Hey there! I'm the co-author and wow I love what you did with it. It's interesting to see how our visions of the SCP differ from what was in my mind when I wrote it. I went with corn stalks growing TV's and you went with cacti growing radio fruit. I hope you do continue working on this and thanks for doing a good job with my first-time ConProcs!

item.title: What a day!
item.content:encoded:
This could very well be my first SCP. About a fruit that makes you explode no less.

Special thanks to Meska for a quick last-minute look at this abomination. And all the other folks on IRC that gave out some cool ideas. This includes fairydoctor and DrFullham.

This needs more work and even if it doesn't last long here, I won't give up and continue working on this article until it could be its Sunday best.

Also, I want to say thanks to my co-author DecoyMesa for the special containment procedure.
